frameworks.name: biologically plausible deep learning
frameworks.one_line: Local learning rules as biologically plausible alternative to backpropagation

frameworks.name: shallow networks with local learning
frameworks.one_line: Single hidden layer trained by unsupervised or fixed projections

frameworks.name: unsupervised feature learning (PCA/ICA/sparse coding)
frameworks.one_line: Local unsupervised rules benchmarked against random and Gabor features

frameworks.name: spiking neural networks with STDP
frameworks.one_line: Leaky integrate-and-fire neurons with spike-timing-dependent plasticity readout

core_question: How far can shallow networks with biologically plausible local learning rules go on standard image classification benchmarks?
key_assumptions: Local learning rules can implement PCA/ICA/sparse coding
Single hidden layer plus readout is a meaningful biologically plausible architecture
manipulations: Hidden layer weights: fixed random vs Gabor vs PCA vs ICA vs sparse coding
All-to-all vs localized receptive field connectivity
key_findings: Unsupervised feature learning does not outperform fixed random or Gabor projections at large hidden-layer sizes
Localized receptive fields significantly outperform all-to-all connectivity
Local-rule networks reach backpropagation-level MNIST performance
Spiking LIF+STDP implementations achieve >98.2% MNIST test accuracy
interpretations: Strong shallow baselines challenge claims of biologically plausible deep learning models on MNIST
MNIST is insufficient to evaluate biologically plausible learning; harder benchmarks are needed
limitations_noted: Shallow architecture limits performance on more complex datasets like CIFAR10
MNIST as the de facto benchmark may be too easy to discriminate models
